segments.text: Everyone loves maidu vada but making
them at home is a total disaster. So
this one Indian company fixed 100 years
of kitchen struggle just by redesigning
a packet. See the problem was not just
the batter. Sure grinding dal is hard.
So ID fresh food sold readymade batter.
But that failed. Why? Because the real
nightmare is the shape. To get the
perfect hole you need wet hands, expert
skills and [music] hot oil. It's messy
and dangerous. And buying that batter
did not fix that mess. So they did not
just sell the food. They designed a
tool. They added a strange umbrella
nozzle to the packet. It mimics the
human hand. You hold it upside down and
squeeze. The nosle cuts the batter into
a perfect doughut directly into the oil.
So, tell me, is it a genius innovation
or just lazy cooking?
